items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan Status Report
items.summary: The council reviewed progress on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9. This plan signals potential improvements or changes to San José's public library system and services.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled from the April 8 meeting and will be rescheduled for a future date to be announced. Residents interested in this project should watch for a new hearing notice.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project Moves Forward
items.summary: The council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue project, suggesting design or coordination work continues. This infrastructure project addresses traffic and rail safety on a major South Bay corridor.

items.headline: Street Repaving Contract Awarded for 2026 Local Streets Project
items.summary: The city awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project. This represents infrastructure maintenance and repair work across San José's street network.

items.headline: City Council updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring
items.summary: Cupertino is developing an updated Active Transportation Plan that affects how the city prioritizes bike lanes, pedestrian paths, and other non-car infrastructure. The council reviewed Phase 2 of the plan and revised scoring criteria that will determine which projects get funded.

items.headline: City Council approves construction project with new contract award
items.summary: The council adopted plans and specifications for a construction project and awarded a contract, though the agenda does not specify which city infrastructure or facility is being built or improved. Residents may want to follow up on the city website for project details.

items.headline: Housing plan amendment considered for federal funding eligibility
items.summary: The council reviewed a substantial amendment to Milpitas' 2025-2030 housing and community development plan. This change may affect how federal grants are allocated for affordable housing and community programs over the next five years.

items.headline: 65-unit senior apartment complex approved for East St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a nearly $9M senior housing community with 65 units at 678 E St John St, addressing San José's shortage of affordable senior living options. This represents significant new housing supply for an underserved demographic.
